retrieved_docs.title: MALToma -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: Non-gastric MALTomas have also been associated with chronic infectious or inflammatory conditions. Ocular adnexal and pulmonary MALTomas are associated with Chlamydia psittaci infections. Furthermore, deletions and mutations in genes such as PIM1 , cMyc , TNFAIP3 , and MyD88 have been noted. Hypermethylation of TNFAIP3 is also another alteration that may be observed in ocular adnexal, salivary, and thyroid lymphomas. [6] The pro-growth stimulatory environment created by the various cytokines and chemokines released, along with the interaction between B and T cells, contribute to increased B-cell survival. This environment can lead to the emergence of precancerous lymphoepithelial lesions, followed by monoclonal lesions.

retrieved_docs.title: MALToma -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: These translocations lead to the constitutive activation of the NF-κB pathway, which stimulates the release of cytokines and enhances cell survival. Alternatively, MALTomas with t(3:14) lead to the overexpression of FOX1P, a transcriptional suppressor of caspase-dependent pro-apoptotic genes. Other chromosomal aberrations, such as trisomies 3, 12, and 18, have also been reported. Although they may not activate NF-κB directly, they utilize alternative pathways to resist apoptosis and promote proliferation. Moreover, somatic mutations, such as missense and frameshift mutations in cMyc and PIM1 , have also been documented.

retrieved_docs.title: Pimecrolimus -- Mechanism of Action
retrieved_docs.content: Activation of T-lymphocytes starts with the interaction of costimulatory ligands on antigen-presenting cells and T-cell receptors. Intracellular free calcium increases and binds to calmodulin, which activates calcineurin (a protein phosphatase). Calcineurin dephosphorylates the cytoplasmic portion of the transcription factor called nuclear factor of activated T cells (NFAT), which is then transported to the nucleus and contributes to the transcription of several inflammatory cytokines.

retrieved_docs.title: Pimecrolimus -- Mechanism of Action
retrieved_docs.content: TCIs, by definition, inhibit calcineurin. Pimecrolimus achieves this as it binds to the FK506-binding protein (FKBP) and blocks calcineurin’s ability to dephosphorylate NFAT, effectively halting calcineurin-dependent transcription of genes for T-cell activation and production of cytokines such as interleukins 2, 4, and 10 as well as interferon-gamma.

retrieved_docs.title: Biochemistry, Protein Targeting and I Cell Diseases -- Molecular Level -- Transport of Lysosomal Proteins (Enzymes)
retrieved_docs.content: There are 2 types of MPRs named according to their molecular mass. MPR46 (cation dependent/CD) and MPR300 (cation independent/CI) are 46 and 300 kDa, respectively. MPR300 is identical to the human insulin-like growth factor (IGF) II receptor; therefore, it is also referred to as the M6P/IGF II receptor. MPRs are type 1 integral membrane proteins. The receptor-enzyme complex is initially sent to a prelysosomal compartment and then to the lysosome. Upon reaching the lysosome, the acidic intralysosomal pH assists in dissociating the enzymes from the attached MPRs. The detached MPRs are then recycled back to the Golgi for later use. [34] Retromer, Rab9, and TIP47 mediate the recycling process. [5] A small fraction of these functional lysosomal enzymes are exported out of the cell but retrieved by MPRs (mainly MPR300) at the cell membrane and brought back into the cell. [32] [35] The cell surface MPRs increase when treated with M6P, IGF I, IGF II, and tumor-promoting phorbol esters since they activate protein kinase C. The quantity of the same receptors decreases when treated with weak bases. [37] In the study by DiCioccio et al, which focused on pseudo-Hurler polydystrophy and ICD, there were normal intracellular and intralysosomal concentrations of specific lysosomal enzymes. This led to the investigation of alternate trafficking pathways, including the M6P-independent path. Both diseases are due to decreased levels of GlcNAc-1-phosphotransferase. DiCioccio et al found that most enzymes in ICD and pseudo-Hurler polydystrophy cells, which did not have the M6P signal, were still localized to the lysosome. [38] Cells like hepatocytes, Kupffer cells, leukocytes, and organs such as the liver, kidney, and brain have near-normal levels of lysosomal enzymes in ICD. Acid phosphatase and beta-glucocerebrosidase do not use the M6P-dependent pathway in any cell type. [39]

retrieved_docs.title: Genetics, Philadelphia Chromosome -- Mechanism
retrieved_docs.content: MAPK/ERK (RAS/RAF/MEK/ERK) pathway : This pathway is involved in signal transmission from cell surface receptors to nuclear factors involved in transcription. Osteopontin (OPN), which overexpresses in BCR/ABL1, uses this pathway to maintain a leukemic microenvironment. It, in turn, promotes and maintains the proliferation and survival of leukemic cells as well as plays a role in cell differentiation and apoptosis. [4] [18]

retrieved_docs.title: Calcineurin Inhibitors -- Mechanism of Action
retrieved_docs.content: The following points elucidate the mechanisms of action and distinct characteristics of various CNIs, such as cyclosporine, tacrolimus, pimecrolimus, and voclosporin, offering valuable insights into their roles in managing diverse conditions: Cyclosporine binds to cyclophilin-1 inside the cells to form the cyclosporine-cyclophilin complex. Subsequently, this complex inhibits calcineurin, which stops the activation and dephosphorylation of NFAT, a factor that plays a crucial role in triggering inflammatory reactions. [14] Tacrolimus and pimecrolimus share a common mechanism of action. Both compounds attach to FK-binding protein, thereby triggering the inhibition of calcineurin.

retrieved_docs.title: Central Nervous System Lymphoma -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: Several mutations in tumor suppressor and proto-oncogenes involved in B-cell activation, differentiation, and apoptosis likely contribute to the development of PCNSL. Somatic hypermutations in proto-oncogenes, such as MYC , PAX5 , Rho/TTF , PIM1 , and tumor suppressor genes, such as PRDM1 , have been demonstrated in PCNSL cases. Nuclear factor kappa B (NF-κB) signaling is believed to play a role in disease pathogenesis. The up-regulation of activators within the NF-κB pathway, such as myeloid differentiation primary response 88 (MYD88), aspase recruitment domain family member 11 (CADR11), and cluster of differentiation 79 (CD79), and suppression of NF-κB inhibitors, such as tumor necrosis factor, alpha-induced protein 3 (TNFAIP3), have been noted in individuals with PCNSL. [5] Aberrant regulation of the Janus kinases (JAKs), and signal transducer and activator of transcription proteins (STATs) (eg, JAK/STAT) pathway is also implicated. Increased levels of interleukin (IL)-10, A JAK/STAT mediator, are seen in CSF analysis of patients with PCNSL and are often associated with a worse prognosis. The expression of MYD88 and CD79B mutations in secondary CNS lymphoma is considerably lower than in primary CNS lymphoma, suggesting a different pathogenesis mechanism. [14]

retrieved_docs.title: Lung Metastasis -- Pathophysiology -- Intrinsic Cell Determinants
retrieved_docs.content: SSeCKS (scaffold protein src-suppressed C-kinase substrate complex) dysregulation occurs in lung metastases from melanoma. It is known to control metastasis-associated protein kinase C (PKC) and SRC (Gene of Rous sarcoma virus) signaling through direct scaffolding activity.

retrieved_docs.title: Calcineurin Inhibitors -- Administration -- Specific Patient Populations
retrieved_docs.content: Pimecrolimus should be applied immediately after nursing, following thorough cleansing of the nipples before lactation. Physicians recommend using a water-soluble cream on the breast, as ointments could expose the infant to elevated levels of mineral paraffin. [25] Due to the insufficient clinical data regarding the presence of voclosporin in breast milk, it is advisable to consider an alternative drug. As per the manufacturer's recommendation, breastfeeding should be avoided while using voclosporin and for at least 1 week following the final dose of the medication. [26]

retrieved_docs.title: Plasmablastic Lymphoma -- Histopathology
retrieved_docs.content: The immunophenotype for plasmablastic lymphoma is also similar to that of plasma cell neoplasms with positive immunohistochemical (IHC) staining for CD79a, IRF-4/MUM-1, CD38, and CD138. Unlike other B-cell lymphomas, PBL is usually negative for CD19, CD20, and PAX-5 but may be weakly positive for CD45. Therefore, immunophenotyping can also aid in the diagnostic evaluation of PBL. [2] PBL can also express T-cell markers CD2 or CD4 in a few cases. MYC overexpression is seen in about half of all cases. The Ki-67 proliferation index is invariably high and often exceeds 90%. [18] The cells can also be positive for CD30, which has implications for using brentuximab vedotin. [18] [19] [18] [20] EBV can be detected by EBV-encoded RNA (EBER) expression in the majority of patients but is widespread in HIV-positive individuals. [21] BCL2 and BCL6 overexpression is not seen with PBL, which can help distinguish it from triple hit lymphoma and Burkitt lymphoma, respectively. [11]

retrieved_docs.title: Calcineurin Inhibitors -- Contraindications -- Pimecrolimus
retrieved_docs.content: Pimecrolimus is contraindicated in patients exhibiting hypersensitivity to the drug or its excipients.

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Tyrosine Kinase Receptors -- Cellular Level
retrieved_docs.content: MAPK/ERK: Mitogen-activated protein kinase/extracellular signal-regulated kinase 1/2 PI3K/Akt/mTOR: Phosphoinositide 3 kinase/Akt/mammalian (or mechanistic) target of rapamycin PLCG1/PKC: Phospholipase C-gamma1/protein kinase C

retrieved_docs.title: Biochemistry, Protein Targeting and I Cell Diseases -- Clinical Significance -- Bony and Radiological Abnormalities
retrieved_docs.content: Dysostosis multiplex is the broad term that encompasses the skeletal abnormalities of ICD. General x-ray findings are demineralization, osteopenia, coarse trabecular bones, and periosteal cloaking (predominantly long bones). [65] [76] X-rays of the upper limbs may show metacarpal pointing and bullet-shaped phalanges. Pelvic radiographs may show iliac flaring. A J-shaped sella turcica may also be visualized. [73] Bone dysplasia with shortened and curved bones is considered a prenatal manifestation of ICD. Deformities of the vertebral bodies may show anterior beaking, leading to abnormal spinal curvatures. [33] The cervical spine vertebral bodies are normal, whereas the dorsal and lumbar spine vertebral bodies have a reduced anteroposterior diameter. In ICD, the bones of the upper limb are more affected than those of the lower limb. The hypoplastic carpal bones and normal tarsal bones exemplify this. [76]

retrieved_docs.title: ALK Negative Anaplastic Large Cell Lymphoma -- Differential Diagnosis
retrieved_docs.content: There is no specific immunophenotypic or genetic features to define ALK(-) ALCL; the immunophenotype of primary cutaneous and systemic ALK(-) ALCL are indistinguishable [8] . Both have strong expression of CD30 with variable expression of T-cell antigens (CD2, CD3, and CD5). Both often express CD4 and cytotoxic proteins. Primary cutaneous ALCL expresses cutaneous lymphocytes antigen more frequently and EMA less frequently than ALK(-) ALCL, but these markers are not specific enough to distinguish these entities by phenotype alone [8] [25] . At present, both entities share recurrent genetic abnormalities, of which the most common are DUSP22 rearrangements which occur in about 28% of cases of primary cutaneous ALCL and 30% of systemic ALK(-) ALCL which may secondarily involve the skin. Clinical staging remains necessary to distinguish DUSP22 rearranged cases of primary cutaneous ALCL from systemic ALK(-) ALCL with DUSP22 rearrangement and skin involvement. ALK-positive ALCL is molecularly characterized and can be readily diagnosed with the highly specific combination of CD30 and ALK positivity.

retrieved_docs.title: Calcineurin Inhibitors -- Monitoring -- Pimecrolimus
retrieved_docs.content: Pimecrolimus is metabolized through the CYP3A enzyme pathway, necessitating vigilant monitoring for potential interactions. The FDA advises patients using this medication to avoid tanning beds, sun lamps, and UV light therapy. In addition, it is recommended that patients opt for regular clothing rather than bandages or dressings that cover the skin.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1286
retrieved_docs.content: Imatinib is a BCR‑ABL inhibitor, meaning it belongs to the class of drugs that specifically target BCR‑ABL fusion proteins. BCR‑ABL fusion proteins serve as the molecular target for BCR‑ABL inhibitors; they are oncogenic drivers created by the Philadelphia chromosome translocation and are central to the pathogenesis of chronic myeloid leukemia and certain acute lymphoblastic leukemias. By binding to and inhibiting these fusion proteins, imatinib and other BCR‑ABL inhibitors can suppress the abnormal signaling that promotes uncontrolled cell proliferation, thereby providing an effective targeted therapy for patients with these malignancies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 16 -- Level 1 -- Community 242
retrieved_docs.content: category, highlighting the integration of diverse compounds in lipid‑lowering strategies. Overall, the knowledge network interconnects cholesterol‑lowering drugs, hematologic therapies, BTK and EGFR‑targeted kinase inhibitors, and hypolipidemic substances under overlapping classifications of protein kinase inhibition, antineoplastic activity, and immunomodulation, underscoring their combined relevance to cardiovascular, oncologic, and hematologic patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 1 -- Community 81
retrieved_docs.content: Blood cancer (ابْيضَاضُ الدَّم) encompasses leukemias, including lymphoid (ابيضاض الدم اللمفاوي) and myeloid (ابيضاض الدم النقوي) forms, with specific subtypes such as chronic lymphocytic leukemia (سرطان الخلايا الليمفاوية المزمن) and acute myeloid leukemia (سرطان الدم النخاعي الحاد). Targeted kinase inhibition is a central therapeutic strategy. BCR‑ABL inhibitors—dasatinib, dasatinib anhydrous, imatinib, ponatinib, ponatinib hydrochloride, and asciminib—are employed, the first three for lymphoid leukemia and the latter three for myeloid leukemia. BTK inhibitors, namely ibrutinib and zanubrutinib, belong to the same protein‑kinase inhibitor class, with ibrutinib indicated for chronic lymphocytic leukemia. Additional protein‑kinase inhibitors with antineoplastic and immunomodulating activity include EGFR inhibitors canertinib dihydrochloride and margetuximab, as well as cabozantinib, gilteritinib, and midostaurin; gilteritinib and midostaurin are specifically used in acute myeloid leukemia. Conventional cytotoxic and corticosteroid agents such as cytarabine, dexamethasone acetate, dexamethasone phosphate, dexamethasonisonikotinát, irinotecan hydrochloride, pipobroman, and prednisolone sodium metasulphobenzoate, prednisolone sodium phosphate, and prednisolone tebutate also have activity against various blood cancers. Dexamethasone acetate additionally serves in the management of septic shock. Conversely, viral agents such as human herpesvirus‑1 and the oncolytic therapy talimogene laherparepvec are contraindicated in patients with blood cancer. These interrelated disease classifications, drug classes, and contraindications illustrate the complexity of therapeutic decision‑making in hematologic malignancies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 1 -- Community 70
retrieved_docs.content: BCR‑ABL inhibitors, a subclass of protein kinase inhibitors with antineoplastic and immunomodulating properties, include dasatinib, dasatinib anhydrous, imatinib, ponatinib, ponatinib hydrochloride and asciminib. Dasatinib, dasatinib anhydrous and imatinib are indicated for lymphoid leukemia, whereas ponatinib, ponatinib hydrochloride and asciminib are indicated for myeloid leukemia. Bruton's tyrosine kinase (BTK) inhibitors, also protein kinase inhibitors with antineoplastic and immunomodulating activity, comprise ibrutinib and zanubrutinib; ibrutinib is used to treat chronic lymphocytic leukemia. Epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR) inhibitors, another protein kinase inhibitor subclass, consist of canertinib dihydrochloride and margetuximab. By blocking EGFR tyrosine‑kinase signaling, these agents disrupt tumor cell proliferation and survival and can modulate immune responses, providing antineoplastic and immunomodulating effects. Collectively, targeted inhibition of BCR‑ABL, BTK, and EGFR exemplifies precision oncology, linking specific molecular targets to distinct hematologic and solid malignancies and guiding patient‑centered cancer therapy.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 19 -- Level 4 -- Community 2916
retrieved_docs.content: Dasatinib anhydrous and imatinib are both classified as BCR‑ABL inhibitors. As BCR‑ABL inhibitors, these agents specifically target the BCR‑ABL fusion protein, a constitutively active tyrosine kinase that drives the development of chronic myeloid leukemia and certain acute lymphoblastic leukemias. By inhibiting BCR‑ABL activity, dasatinib anhydrous and imatinib disrupt downstream signaling pathways, reducing malignant cell proliferation and providing therapeutic benefit in BCR‑ABL–positive hematologic malignancies. Their shared classification highlights a common mechanism of action, while differences in potency, spectrum of activity, and side‑effect profiles inform clinicians’ choices of targeted therapy for patients with BCR‑ABL–driven disease.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 19 -- Level 4 -- Community 2917
retrieved_docs.content: Dasatinib and imatinib mesylát are therapeutic agents that may be used to treat ابيضاض الدم اللمفاوي, a form of lymphoid leukemia. Both drugs target abnormal tyrosine‑kinase activity characteristic of this hematologic malignancy, providing a targeted approach to disease control and highlighting the role of kinase inhibitors in modern leukemia treatment.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 3 -- Community 1504
retrieved_docs.content: Dasatinib and imatinib are both tyrosine‑kinase inhibitor medications that may be used to treat ابيضاض الدم اللمفاوي, a form of lymphoid leukemia. Both agents target abnormal signaling pathways in malignant lymphoid cells, offering therapeutic options for patients with this hematologic malignancy. Their use reflects the importance of targeted therapy in managing lymphoid leukemias and underscores the need for individualized treatment planning.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 3 -- Community 1437
retrieved_docs.content: Imatinib, also known as imatinib mesylate, is a BCR‑ABL inhibitor; it belongs to the class of agents that target the BCR‑ABL tyrosine kinase and is used therapeutically to inhibit this oncogenic driver in relevant hematologic malignancies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 19 -- Level 1 -- Community 214
retrieved_docs.content: The community describes blood cancer (ابْيضَاضُ الدَّم) as a group of leukemic disorders comprising lymphoid leukemia (ابيضاض الدم اللمفاوي) and myeloid leukemia (ابيضاض الدم النقوي), which correspond to chronic lymphocytic leukemia (سرطان الخلايا الليمفاوية المزمن) and acute myeloid leukemia (سرطان الدم النخاعي الحاد), respectively. Targeted therapy relies heavily on protein kinase inhibitors, antineoplastic and immunomodulating agents. BCR‑ABL inhibitors—dasatinib, dasatinib anhydrous, imatinib, imatinib mesylát, ponatinib, ponatinib hydrochloride, and asciminib—block the BCR‑ABL tyrosine kinase; the former four are used for lymphoid leukemia, whereas ponatinib, ponatinib hydrochloride, and asciminib are indicated for myeloid leukemia. Bruton's tyrosine kinase inhibitors ibrutinib and zanubrutinib also belong to this class, with ibrutinib approved for chronic lymphocytic leukemia. Additional protein kinase inhibitors include EGFR inhibitors canertinib dihydrochloride and margetuximab, as well as other ATC‑listed agents cabozantinib, gilteritinib, and midostaurin; gilteritinib and midostaurin are employed in acute myeloid leukemia. Corticosteroids such as dexamethasone acetate (used for blood cancer and septic shock), dexamethasone phosphate, prednisolone sodium metasulphobenzoate, prednisolone sodium phosphate, and prednisolone tebutate demonstrate efficacy in hematologic malignancies. Chemotherapeutic agents irinotecan hydrochloride and pipobroman are also utilized. Contraindicated interventions include infection with human herpesvirus 1 and oncolytic viral therapy talimogene laherparepvec, which may exacerbate the disease. Collectively, these agents illustrate the role of kinase‑targeted and immunomodulatory drugs in the comprehensive management of leukemic disorders.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1287
retrieved_docs.content: Dasatinib and its anhydrous form, dasatinib anhydrous, are both identified as potential therapeutic agents for treating ابيضاض الدم اللمفاوي, indicating that this tyrosine‑kinase inhibitor and its dehydrated variant may be used in the management of this lymphoid leukemia.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 16 -- Level 3 -- Community 2646
retrieved_docs.content: Dasatinib and dasatinib anhydrous are both classified as BCR‑ABL inhibitors, a class of targeted therapies that block the BCR‑ABL tyrosine kinase activity implicated in certain leukemias. By inhibiting this oncogenic kinase, both the standard and anhydrous formulations of dasatinib help to control malignant cell proliferation and are used in the management of chronic myeloid leukemia and Philadelphia chromosome‑positive acute lymphoblastic leukemia. Recognizing dasatinib and its anhydrous form as BCR‑ABL inhibitors underscores their shared mechanism of action and therapeutic role in precision oncology.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1288
retrieved_docs.content: Bruton's tyrosine kinase (BTK) inhibitors constitute a subclass of protein kinase inhibitors that possess antineoplastic and immunomodulating activity. Within this therapeutic class, zanubrutinib is a specific BTK inhibitor that exemplifies the targeted approach of these agents. By inhibiting BTK, these drugs are employed in the treatment of certain malignancies and immune‑mediated conditions, reflecting their role as both anticancer and immune‑modulating therapies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 1768
retrieved_docs.content: BCR-ABL Inhibitor is a class of targeted therapy that includes ponatinib, ponatinib hydrochloride, and asciminib. Ponatinib and its hydrochloride form, as well as asciminib, are members of the BCR-ABL Inhibitor class and are employed in the treatment of أبيضاض الدم النقوي, a myeloid leukemia. These agents act by inhibiting the BCR‑ABL tyrosine kinase, thereby controlling disease progression. Their shared mechanism and therapeutic indication underscore the importance of BCR‑ABL inhibition in managing this hematologic malignancy.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 1767
retrieved_docs.content: Dasatinib, including its anhydrous formulation dasatinib anhydrous, and imatinib are all agents that may be used to treat ابيضاض الدم اللمفاوي, a form of lymphoid leukemia. These medications, which function as tyrosine‑kinase inhibitors, are employed in therapeutic regimens aimed at controlling the proliferation of malignant lymphoid cells in patients with this hematologic malignancy.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1289
retrieved_docs.content: Ibrutinib is a targeted therapeutic agent that may be used to treat سرطـان الخلايا الليمفاوية المزمن (chronic lymphocytic leukemia), offering a pharmacologic option for managing this hematologic malignancy.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 2 -- Community 483
retrieved_docs.content: Bruton's tyrosine kinase (BTK) inhibitors constitute a subclass of protein kinase inhibitors, antineoplastic and immunomodulating agents, and this class includes the drugs ibrutinib and zanubrutinib. Ibrutinib, as a member of the BTK inhibitor group, may be used to treat سرطان الخلايا الليمفاوية المزمن (chronic lymphocytic leukemia). Similarly, Epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR) inhibitors are also classified within the broader category of protein kinase inhibitors, antineoplastic and immunomodulating agents; this EGFR inhibitor group comprises agents such as canertinib dihydrochloride and margetuximab. Together, BTK inhibitors and EGFR inhibitors illustrate how distinct targeted therapies share common mechanistic foundations as protein kinase inhibitors with antineoplastic and immunomodulatory effects, underscoring their relevance in the treatment of various cancers and immune‑related conditions.
